mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146730 thousand as of August 2017 shows sustained employment growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.2% year-over-year, the Fed enters a tightening regime that compresses equity valuations. This interaction between rising rates and accelerating inflation produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.16% and CPIAUCSL at 245.03 as of August 2017
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the interaction signal, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 3% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,100 thousand units, signaling weakening consumer and residential demand that portends lower corporate earnings in the next quarter, producing negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 3% AND HOUST < 1100 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 474841 million and HOUST at 1155 thousand as of July 2017
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, indicating strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.7% in a single month, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off behavior and reduce forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in one month
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above 4% month-over-month, indicating broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues and equity multiples, producing positive 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND RSAFS MoM growth > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17030 billion (2017 dollars) as of April 2017, RSAFS at 474841 million as of July 2017
regime_hint: synchronized demand acceleration across consumption and output
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays below 2.0% year-over-year, indicating an accommodative monetary stance that supports equity valuations through low discount rates and positive real rate expectations, producing positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.16% and CPIAUCSL at 245.03 as of August 2017
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a supply shock combined with labor market weakness that reduces earnings capacity and equity valuations, producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline > 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.739 and UNRATE at 4.4% as of August 2017
regime_hint: supply shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
